text:
Market today: The mood for tech and chips is sour, driven by disappointing earnings from Netflix and AI struggles at Alphabet. This puts pressure on your concentrated tech/chip book.

Your holdings: SOXL (-44.02%) and SPAL (-41.51%) are in deep drawdown and need immediate attention due to their leveraged nature and decay. SMH (-10.6%) and QQQ (-1.22%) also show losses and are flagged for trim. Remember, margin amplifies these drops.

Watch: Keep a close eye on the semiconductor sector given the "bear market" talk. Any further weakness there will hit your core holdings hard.

Bottom line: Prioritize aggressive risk management and consider reducing exposure in your most vulnerable, leveraged positions.
plays: SOXL: Review your stop for SOXL immediately; consider cutting losses given the -44.02% drawdown and "SELL" status.
SPAL: Address your SPAL position; its -41.51% loss in a leveraged ETF demands a clear exit strategy or tighter stop.
No new buys today - market headwinds for tech and chips suggest caution.
